name: Guardrails AI
allowed_use_boundary: Use as model input/output validation and correction crosswalk evidence.
Do not treat a validator pass or block as execution authority or certification.
claims: Guardrails AI provides programmable validators and guardrails for validating, constraining, and correcting model inputs and outputs.
non_claims: Guardrails AI does not prove policy legitimacy.
A validator pass does not establish actor authority or commit-time standing.
A guardrail block is not StegVerse certification.

transition_table_mapping.claims: programmable validation, constraint, and correction of model inputs and outputs
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no policy legitimacy, standing, certification, or execution authority
transition_table_mapping.input_artifact_type: model input, output, validator configuration, or guardrail configuration
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: configured runtime actors; authority not established by validator result
transition_table_mapping.evidence_model: official project source plus local bounded crosswalk
transition_table_mapping.decision_or_result_model: pass, fail, correction, or block evidence only
transition_table_mapping.receipt_or_trace_model: validator output and local compatibility record
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: configuration and output may reconstruct bounded guardrail behavior
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: guardrail evidence may inform standing and policy review
transition_table_mapping.StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap: preparation, execution-boundary, and semantic review evidence
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: missing configuration, unknown validator coverage, missing runtime context, or authority overclaim

fail_closed_conditions: source reference or version is missing
validator or rail configuration is absent
behavior is generalized beyond observed configuration
comparative behavior is claimed without executable evidence
